evidence: State objective targets replacing GitHub Copilot with live tab-style autocomplete in both VS Code and Obsidian.md.
State objective explicitly prioritizes existing GPT or Claude subscriptions where possible and includes open-source options.
Project AGENTS.md describes the project as an alternative to Copilot autocomplete supporting either GPT or Claude subscription.
captures_request.caveat: I did not inspect any original user prompt outside the provided orchestration state and project instruction files, so exact fidelity to the initial request is inferred rather than independently proven.

evidence: The state defines a single JSON final report under reports/ with options, constraints, recommendations, sources, and confidence levels.
The queue includes discovery of subscription constraints, VS Code options, Obsidian options, self-hosted/open-source architecture, synthesis, and independent verification.
The state requires current web research, primary sources, independent verification, valid JSON, and source-backed claims.
The task specs require source URLs and confidence levels but do not define a common claim/source schema, which may make synthesis and verification harder.

weak_assumptions.assumption: Existing ChatGPT or Claude subscriptions may be usable directly for tab-style autocomplete.
weak_assumptions.risk: Consumer/team subscriptions often differ from API access; unsupported browser/session automation may be brittle or violate terms.
weak_assumptions.recommendation: Keep T010 as a hard dependency for all recommendation language about subscription reuse.

weak_assumptions.assumption: Obsidian has true Copilot-like live inline/tab autocomplete options comparable to VS Code.
weak_assumptions.risk: Many Obsidian AI plugins are chat, command, or selection-generation tools rather than low-latency inline suggestions accepted by Tab.
weak_assumptions.recommendation: Require T030 to classify each Obsidian option by interaction mode: true inline suggestion, completion popup, command generation, chat, or custom-plugin-only.

weak_assumptions.assumption: General GPT/Claude chat models are good substitutes for Copilot-style autocomplete.
weak_assumptions.risk: Autocomplete quality and latency depend on FIM support, context shaping, caching, debouncing, streaming behavior, and model serving latency; chat-quality alone is insufficient.
weak_assumptions.recommendation: Have VS Code and architecture workers explicitly evaluate inline/FIM support, acceptance UX, latency expectations, and context handling.

weak_assumptions.assumption: The final recommendation can be reliable without knowing the user's local constraints.
weak_assumptions.risk: Best option may depend on OS, hardware/GPU, willingness to pay API usage, privacy requirements, and exact subscription tier.
weak_assumptions.recommendation: Report should include a decision tree and list assumptions rather than pretending one universal answer.

weak_assumptions.assumption: Workers will have current web access.
weak_assumptions.risk: The quality gates require current web sources; without web access, subscription/product status claims cannot be verified.
weak_assumptions.recommendation: Orchestrator should confirm web-research capability before dispatching T010-T040 or mark a blocker and request source-gathering help.

recommended_task_changes.change: Add explicit output fields for subscription_tier, includes_api_access, direct_extension_use_supported, requires_separate_api_billing, unofficial_session_automation_risk, source_urls, and confidence.
recommended_task_changes.reason: This prevents ambiguous answers about 'using a subscription' versus using paid API credentials.

recommended_task_changes.change: Ensure the candidate set includes commercial baselines as well as open-source projects, and explicitly marks VS Code forks or non-extension products as near-scope if they do not run inside VS Code.
recommended_task_changes.reason: A practical Copilot replacement comparison needs baselines such as Tabnine, Codeium/Windsurf, Supermaven, Sourcegraph Cody, Continue, Tabby, and twinny where current evidence supports inclusion.

recommended_task_changes.change: Require a mode taxonomy for every Obsidian plugin: true live inline/tab autocomplete, editor completion popup, command/selection generation, chat/sidebar, or not applicable.
recommended_task_changes.reason: The central UX requirement is live tab-style autocomplete, not generic AI writing assistance.

recommended_task_changes.change: Add separate feasibility ratings for local-only, API-key, OpenAI-compatible proxy, and unofficial subscription/session-proxy architectures.
recommended_task_changes.reason: The architecture answer should separate practical supported paths from brittle or policy-risk paths.
The synthesis worker should incorporate this critique and any added quality gates.

recommended_task_changes.change: Add reports/orchestration/autocomplete-research-state.json and reports/orchestration/handoffs/T000-init-state.json to read_first or read_if_needed.
recommended_task_changes.reason: Verification should check the final report against the objective and initial critique, not only against the synthesized report.

recommended_new_tasks.title: Confirm web-research capability and source capture protocol
recommended_new_tasks.scope: Before substantive research, confirm workers can access current web sources and standardize how they record source URLs, dates, claim support, and confidence.
success_criteria: Either confirm current web access for T010-T040 or record a blocker.
Define a minimal common JSON schema for claims, options, sources, and confidence used by all research handoffs.
recommended_new_tasks.reason: Current web evidence is a hard quality gate, and inconsistent handoff schemas will make synthesis/verification weaker.

recommended_new_tasks.title: Check final report schema and claim traceability before independent factual verification
recommended_new_tasks.scope: Validate JSON parseability, required sections, and that major recommendations trace to source-backed worker findings before T060 performs factual checks.
success_criteria: Final JSON parses with jq or equivalent.
Every major recommendation has either source-backed evidence or is explicitly labeled inference/speculation.
Both VS Code and Obsidian are addressed separately and jointly.
recommended_new_tasks.reason: This catches structural issues cheaply before the independent verifier spends time on factual audit.

recommended_new_tasks.title: Patch final report if independent verification fails
recommended_new_tasks.scope: Apply minimal corrections identified by T060, then spawn a re-verification task.
success_criteria: Corrections from T060 are applied or explicitly rejected with rationale.
A follow-up independent verification task is queued if substantive changes are made.
recommended_new_tasks.reason: The current queue has no explicit repair path if T060 fails or finds material errors.

orchestrator_notes: No blocker prevents dispatching T010-T040 if web-capable workers are available.
Update the orchestration state after ingesting this handoff: mark T000 complete, add this file to artifacts.handoffs, and consider adding T005/T055/T070 or folding their criteria into existing tasks.
The final task should remain independent verification; if T060 edits the final report materially, a separate re-verification task should be queued.
